sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.13 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: It is known that the group$^$has a subgroup whose dual is free abelian of rank$2^{ _0}$(see 17.24 in Archive). Does$^$have a subgroup whose dual is free abelian of still larger rank (the largest possible being$2^{2^{ _0}}$)? This is Question~11 in (G.\,M.\,Bergman, Portugaliae Math., { 69} (2012) 69--84).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
